crons.name: heartbeat
crons.prompt: Read HEARTBEAT.md and follow its instructions. Update your heartbeat, check inbox, and work on your highest priority task.

crons.name: usage-monitor
crons.prompt: Check Claude API usage: run bash "${CTX_FRAMEWORK_ROOT}/bus/check-usage-api.sh" --warn-7day 80 --warn-5h 90 2>&1. The script itself sends the hard threshold alerts to Cody via CTX_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID (7-day >= 80% CODE RED, 5-hour >= 90% warning, Codex limit/expiry) — do NOT re-send those. Then parse the JSON output and: (1) For 7-day, if utilization crossed a new 10% increment since the last check (10/20/30/.../90%), notify Cody: cortextos bus send-telegram $CTX_TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID '7-day usage: X% (resets <date>)'. Read the last logged 7-day value from today's memory file to compare. Apply the same 10%-increment soft-notify to codex.utilization_5h and codex.utilization_7d. (2) Always log the current Claude + Codex levels in your daily memory.

crons.name: nightly-metrics
crons.prompt: Run nightly metrics collection: cortextos bus collect-metrics. Review the output and report any anomalies via Telegram to the orchestrator.

crons.name: auto-commit
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/local-version-control/SKILL.md and execute the daily version control workflow. Stage, review, and commit changes.

crons.name: check-upstream
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/upstream-sync/SKILL.md and execute the upstream sync workflow. Check for framework updates and report to the orchestrator.

crons.name: catalog-browse
crons.prompt: Read .claude/skills/catalog-browse/SKILL.md and execute the catalog browse workflow. Check for new community skills and recommend useful ones.
